metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the `pass1.py` line citation for the per-criterion fail-open from `:453` (the systemic `LLMUnavailableError` re-raise) to `:458` (the `except Exception` that drops chunk findings and continues).
findings.finding: The plan cites `pass1.py:453` as the per-criterion fail-open (one flaky finder marks criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues), but line 453 is `except LLMUnavailableError: raise` â€” the SYSTEMIC re-raise that aborts the whole tier. The actual per-criterion fail-open (drops chunk findings and continues) is at line 458.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the per-criterion fail-open is a PLAN-REVIEW property (one flaky finder among N marks that criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues â€” `pass1.py:453`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change `pass1.py:453` to `pass1.py:458` in the Scope section and in any `# CURRENT(pass1.py:453)` annotation in the test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the pydantic-ai exception types named for 'structured content-filter refusal' and 'incomplete-tool-call error' exist in `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at the installed version, or replace them with the correct rebar-level exception (`UnretryableOutputError` / `StructuredOutputError`) and the correct injection mechanism (canned `finish_reason` via `FunctionModel`, not a raised library exception).
findings.finding: The plan asserts that 'structured provider content-filter refusal' and 'incomplete-tool-call error' are injectable via exception types importable from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`, but no such symbols (`ContentFilterError`, `IncompleteToolCall`, or analogues) exist anywhere in the codebase or its pydantic-ai usage. The only symbol imported from `pydantic_ai.exceptions` in rebar is `UsageLimitExceeded`.
findings.location: Scope section: 'a structured provider content-filter refusal, an incomplete-tool-call error by raising the matching pydantic-ai exception types (the test imports those types from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the failure-matrix error-type column for `content_filter`, `unparseable output`, and `validation-retry exhaustion`: these raise `UnretryableOutputError` or `StructuredOutputError` (via `except LLMError: raise` at runner.py:351), NOT `LLMUnavailableError`, and the matrix and `# CURRENT()` annotations must reflect this.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `content_filter` finish_reason, `unparseable output`, and `validation-retry exhaustion` failures 'collapse to `LLMUnavailableError`' at the broad `except Exception` seam (runner.py:353-365), but the code shows these paths raise `UnretryableOutputError` / `StructuredOutputError` (both `LLMError` subclasses), which are caught by `except LLMError: raise` at line 351 and pass through UNCHANGED â€” not remapped to `LLMUnavailableError`.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Provider failures (HTTP status, timeout, connect, content-filter, unparseable, validation-exhaustion) collapse to `LLMUnavailableError` at the broad `except Exception` seam (runner.py:353-365)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the matrix to assert `UnretryableOutputError` for content_filter/length/truncation cases, `StructuredOutputError` for unparseable/validation-exhaustion cases, and reserve `LLMUnavailableError` only for HTTP-status, timeout, and connect failures that reach the bare `except Exception` at line 353.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `ContentFilterError` and `IncompleteToolCall` exist in `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at version 1.107.0 (e.g. `python -c "from pydantic_ai.exceptions import ContentFilterError, IncompleteToolCall"`); if absent, replace each with the actual pydantic-ai symbol (likely `UnexpectedModelBehavior` for content-filter/incomplete-tool-call) or use a plain `Exception` subclass injected directly.
findings.finding: The plan names `ContentFilterError` and `IncompleteToolCall` as distinct pydantic-ai exception classes importable from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`, but neither symbol appears anywhere in the repo's codebase imports or pydantic-ai usage â€” only `UsageLimitExceeded` and `ModelHTTPError` are verified pydantic_ai exception imports. If these symbols don't exist in pydantic-ai 1.107.0, the test file will fail at collection with an ImportError.
findings.location: Scope section â€” failure matrix bullets: 'a structured provider content-filter refusal, an incomplete-tool-call error' / 'the test imports those types from pydantic_ai.exceptions'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `ContentFilterError` with `UnexpectedModelBehavior` (verified present in pydantic-ai docs/ADR) and `IncompleteToolCall` with `UnexpectedModelBehavior` or remove as a distinct case. Import `from pydantic_ai.exceptions import UnexpectedModelBehavior` instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `pass1.py` citation in the Scope from `:453` to `:458` (the `except Exception` per-chunk fail-open that silently drops findings) to match the actual per-criterion fail-open seam.
findings.finding: The plan cites `pass1.py:453` as the 'per-criterion fail-open' seam where 'one flaky finder among N marks that criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues', but line 453 of `pass1.py` is `except LLMUnavailableError: raise` â€” the SYSTEMIC re-raise that aborts the fan-out. The actual per-criterion fail-open is at line 458 (`except Exception: ... chunk_errors += 1`), which silently drops findings without aborting.
findings.location: Scope section: 'per-criterion fail-open is a PLAN-REVIEW property (one flaky finder among N marks that criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues â€” pass1.py:453)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'pass1.py:453' to 'pass1.py:458' in the Scope section, and update the `# CURRENT(pass1.py:453)` annotation in the test to `# CURRENT(pass1.py:458)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the error-type classification in the Scope section and AC-3: `content_filter` finish_reason, `length`/`max_tokens` truncation, `refusal`, and validation-retry exhaustion currently raise `UnretryableOutputError` / `StructuredOutputError` (both `LLMRunnerError` subclasses) via `except LLMError: raise` at runner.py:351 â€” they do NOT reach the `except Exception â†’ LLMUnavailableError` seam at runner.py:353; the matrix must assert the correct current type for each.
findings.finding: The plan incorrectly classifies `content_filter` finish_reason, `length`/`max_tokens` truncation, `refusal`, and `validation-retry exhaustion` as collapsing to `LLMUnavailableError` at the broad `except Exception` seam. In the actual code, `structured.check_stop_reason()` (runner.py:483) raises `UnretryableOutputError` (a subclass of `StructuredOutputError â†’ LLMRunnerError`) for `content_filter`, `length`, `max_tokens`, and `refusal`; and exhausted validation retry raises `StructuredOutputError` (also `LLMRunnerError`). Both exit via `except LLMError: raise` at runner.py:351-352 â€” they NEVER reach the `except Exception` seam at runner.py:353. If the test pins these as `LLMUnavailableError`, the suite will fail on today's code, contradicting AC-1 ('make test runs it green on TODAY's code').
findings.location: Scope section, 'Two current-behavior error types, kept distinct' paragraph; also AC-3
findings.suggested_fix: Partition the 'provider failures' bucket into three sub-groups: (1) HTTP-status / timeout / connect / unknown â†’ `LLMUnavailableError` (via `except Exception` at runner.py:353-365); (2) `content_filter` / `refusal` / `length` / `max_tokens` finish_reason â†’ `UnretryableOutputError` (via `except LLMError: raise` at runner.py:351, raised from `structured.check_stop_reason`); (3) unparseable-output / validation-retry-exhaustion â†’ `StructuredOutputError` (also via `except LLMError: raise`). AC-3 and every `# CURRENT(...)` comment must reflect these three distinct paths.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `ContentFilterError` and `IncompleteToolCall` are real importable symbols in `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at v1.107.0; if they do not exist, replace with the actual pydantic-ai symbol(s) for content-filter and incomplete-tool-call conditions (e.g. `UnexpectedModelBehavior` with the appropriate message, or a `finish_reason='content_filter'` canned response) and update the Scope description to name the concrete injection mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'a structured provider content-filter refusal' and 'an incomplete-tool-call error' are injectable 'by raising the matching pydantic-ai exception types (the test imports those types from pydantic_ai.exceptions)', implying named symbols ContentFilterError and IncompleteToolCall exist in pydantic_ai.exceptions at v1.107.0. Neither symbol appears anywhere in src/, tests/, the codebase's own pydantic-ai imports (only ModelHTTPError, UsageLimitExceeded, UnexpectedModelBehavior are evidenced), or the pydantic-ai documentation surface visible in the repo's research notes. The codebase's own content_filter path is finish_reason-string-driven (structured.py:219,229), not exception-class-driven. If these symbols do not exist, any test that imports them fails collection, violating AC#1.
findings.location: Scope section â€” 'a structured provider content-filter refusal, an incomplete-tool-call error â€¦ the test imports those types from pydantic_ai.exceptions'
findings.suggested_fix: For content-filter: inject via a canned FunctionModel returning `finish_reason='content_filter'` (already exercised in structured.py:219 / runner.py:483 via check_stop_reason) rather than a non-existent exception class. For incomplete-tool-call: inject via `UnexpectedModelBehavior` (the documented retry-exhaustion exception at structured-output-research.md:71), or via a FunctionModel that returns a malformed ToolCallPart. Update the Scope bullet to name the actual injection mechanism for each case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the error-type mapping in the Scope / AC#3 for `content_filter` finish_reason, `length`/truncation, and `validation-retry exhaustion` â€” these pass through as `UnretryableOutputError` / `StructuredOutputError` (caught by `except LLMError: raise` at runner.py:351), NOT `LLMUnavailableError`; update the per-class assertion table accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan classifies `content_filter` finish_reason, `length`/truncation finish_reason, and `validation-retry exhaustion` as provider failures that 'collapse to `LLMUnavailableError`' via the `except Exception` seam at runner.py:353â€“365 â€” but these are all `LLMError` subclasses (`UnretryableOutputError` and `StructuredOutputError`) and are caught by the earlier `except LLMError: raise` guard at runner.py:351, passing through UNCHANGED. An implementer following the plan will assert the wrong error type for these matrix entries, causing the 'pinned RED baseline' to fail `make test` on today's code.
findings.location: Scope section â€” 'Two current-behavior error types, kept distinct' bullet; AC#3
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope 'Two current-behavior error types' bullet, add a third category: 'structured-output failures (content_filter finish_reason, length/truncation, validation-retry exhaustion) propagate as UnretryableOutputError or StructuredOutputError (LLMRunnerError subclasses) via the `except LLMError: raise` guard at runner.py:351.' Update AC#3 to list all three categories.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and name the exact `pydantic_ai.exceptions` class names for 'structured provider content-filter refusal' and 'incomplete-tool-call error' at pydantic-ai 1.107.0 (or remove them from the matrix if the symbols do not exist), grounding each with a concrete `from pydantic_ai.exceptions import <ClassName>` that succeeds at that version.
findings.finding: The Scope section specifies that 'a structured provider content-filter refusal' and 'an incomplete-tool-call error' are injected 'by raising the matching pydantic-ai exception types (the test imports those types from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`)' â€” but neither the specific class names nor their verified existence in the pydantic-ai 1.107.0 public API are provided. No such symbols exist anywhere in the repo. An implementer cannot write these two parametrized cases without knowing the actual class names.
findings.location: Scope section â€” failure matrix bullet list, 'incomplete-tool-call error' and 'structured provider content-filter refusal' entries

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the per-criterion fail-open seam citation from `pass1.py:453` to `pass1.py:458` (the `except Exception` non-systemic chunk-error handler); update AC#2's example CURRENT comment accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan cites `pass1.py:453` as the per-criterion fail-open seam ('one flaky finder among N marks that criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues â€” `pass1.py:453`'), but `pass1.py:453` is `except LLMUnavailableError: raise` â€” a SYSTEMIC re-raise, not a per-criterion fail-open. The actual per-criterion non-systemic fail-open (drop chunk findings and continue) is at `pass1.py:458`. The AC#2 `# CURRENT(pass1.py:453)` comment will pin the wrong seam and mislead the author of later stories.
findings.location: Scope section â€” 'Two-scope scenarios' bullet; AC#2 CURRENT comment convention
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'one flaky finder among N marks that criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues â€” `pass1.py:453`' with '`pass1.py:458`' (the `except Exception` non-systemic handler that drops chunk findings and increments `chunk_errors`).

findings.finding: `ContentFilterError` and `IncompleteToolCall` are named in AC#3 as symbols to be imported from `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at module top (with 'an absent symbol fails collection loudly' as the enforcement), but neither symbol appears anywhere in the rebar codebase â€” no existing test or src file ever imports them from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`. Every other pydantic-ai exception the plan cites (`ModelHTTPError`, `UsageLimitExceeded`) is verifiably present through existing rebar imports. The plan defends these two symbols with a 'Verified importable at pydantic-ai 1.107.0' claim but provides no in-repo grounding. If either symbol is absent from the installed version of pydantic-ai, AC#3's mandatory top-level import causes a collection failure that breaks AC#1 (`make test` green).
findings.location: Scope section (failure matrix bullets for 'ContentFilterError' and 'IncompleteToolCall') and AC#3 ('imported from pydantic_ai.exceptions at module top; an absent symbol fails collection loudly')
findings.suggested_fix: If the symbols are absent, replace the `ContentFilterError` matrix case with a `FunctionModel` that raises `UnexpectedModelBehavior` (confirmed importable from `pydantic_ai.exceptions` per existing docs) with a content-filter-flavored message, and replace `IncompleteToolCall` similarly. Remove both from the AC#3 import list and document the substitution.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that `ContentFilterError` and `IncompleteToolCall` are importable from `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at v1.107.0 and pre-emptively labels any code-grounding absence finding a 'false positive,' but provides no independently verifiable evidence: no committed import smoke-test, no link to the pydantic-ai 1.107.0 changelog/source, no pip-show or test that runs in CI. The only pydantic-ai exceptions import in the codebase (`runtime_pydanticai_poc.py`) uses only `ModelHTTPError`. Multiple prior review passes have flagged these symbols as absent, and AC#3 requires AC fails loudly at collection time if the import fails â€” meaning an incorrect symbol name will break the entire test suite on `make test`.
findings.location: Scope section â€” 'Third-party exception classes are imported, not code-grounded against rebar `src/`' + AC #3

findings.finding: `ContentFilterError` is listed in the failure matrix as a distinct injectable exception class, but no such symbol exists anywhere in the rebar codebase or in pydantic-ai's exception surface (only `ModelHTTPError` and `UsageLimitExceeded` are imported from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`). The `content_filter` signal is implemented solely as a `finish_reason` string value in `structured.py:219`, not as a raiseable exception. The plan's 'symbol-presence assertion at collection time' requirement would therefore cause a hard `ImportError`/`AttributeError` at collection, breaking the entire test file.
findings.location: Scope section: 'ContentFilterError' in failure matrix; AC1: 'Each pydantic-ai symbol asserted to exist as imported'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the `ContentFilterError` entry with a `content_filter` finish-reason injection case: a `FunctionModel` returning a `ModelResponse` with `finish_reason='content_filter'`, asserting `UnretryableOutputError` (structured.py:229) is raised and gate behavior is INDETERMINATE.

findings.finding: `IncompleteToolCall` is named as a discrete failure-matrix entry in the Scope section, but this symbol does not exist anywhere in the rebar codebase (src/ or tests/) and produces zero search hits. It is not a known public symbol in pydantic-ai's exception or message surface. The plan's collection-time symbol-presence assertion requirement means an attempted import would raise `ImportError`, breaking the test file.
findings.location: Scope section: 'IncompleteToolCall' in the failure matrix list

findings.finding: `ContentFilterError` is listed in the Scope failure matrix as a distinct injectable exception class ('ContentFilterError' bullet), but this symbol does not exist in `pydantic_ai.exceptions` or anywhere in src/ or tests/. The content-filter case in pydantic-ai is a `finish_reason='content_filter'` string, not a raised exception. The plan's own AC states 'Each pydantic-ai symbol asserted to exist as imported â€¦ a renamed/absent SDK symbol fails loudly rather than silently skips' â€” meaning a `from pydantic_ai.exceptions import ContentFilterError` would cause test collection to fail immediately.
findings.location: Scope â€” failure matrix bullet: '`content_filter` finish_reason, `ContentFilterError`'

Remove any `from pydantic_ai.exceptions import ContentFilterError` collection-time assertion.

findings.finding: `IncompleteToolCall` is listed as a discrete failure-matrix entry in the Scope section, but this symbol is absent from all of `src/`, `tests/`, and any pydantic-ai import in the codebase. The plan's AC requires a symbol-presence assertion at collection time, which would produce an ImportError if the symbol doesn't exist in pydantic-ai's public API.
findings.location: Scope â€” failure matrix bullet: '`IncompleteToolCall`'

findings.finding: `ContentFilterError` is named in the failure matrix as a pydantic-ai symbol that must be import-asserted at collection time, but this class does not exist in pydantic-ai's public exception surface (only `ModelHTTPError` and `UsageLimitExceeded` are real symbols in `pydantic_ai.exceptions`). An import assertion for a nonexistent symbol will raise ImportError at collection, breaking `make test` on today's code.

findings.finding: `IncompleteToolCall` is listed in the failure matrix as a named pydantic-ai symbol requiring a collection-time import assertion, but this class does not appear anywhere in pydantic-ai's documented public API, the rebar codebase, or any test file. findings.location: Scope section, failure matrix: '`IncompleteToolCall`'

findings.finding: `ContentFilterError` is named as a discrete failure-matrix entry whose symbol presence is asserted at collection time, but no such exception class exists in pydantic-ai's public exception surface or anywhere in the codebase â€” `content_filter` is only a finish_reason string (structured.py:219), not an importable exception.

This matches the actual code path in structured.py:229-243.

findings.finding: `IncompleteToolCall` is named as a discrete failure-matrix entry with a mandatory symbol-presence assertion, but this symbol does not appear anywhere in src/, tests/, or the pydantic-ai POC file, and is absent from the only pydantic-ai exception symbols the codebase actually imports.

findings.finding: `ContentFilterError` is listed as a distinct bullet in the failure matrix with a mandatory symbol-presence assertion at collection time, but this symbol does not exist in `pydantic_ai.exceptions`, anywhere in rebar's codebase, or in pydantic-ai 1.107.0's public API. The `content_filter` concept in rebar is a `finish_reason` string value (`_BAD_STOP_REASONS` key in `structured.py:219`) and raises `UnretryableOutputError` â€” not a `ContentFilterError` exception class. The plan conflates two distinct injection mechanics (raise-an-exception vs inject-a-finish_reason) under one bullet, leaving the executor without a concrete symbol to import.

findings.suggested_fix: Split into: `content_filter finish_reason` (canned FunctionModel returning a response with finish_reason='content_filter', pin: `UnretryableOutputError` raised via `structured.py:243`) and remove the bare `ContentFilterError` exception bullet unless the exact pydantic-ai 1.107.0 import path is confirmed.

findings.finding: `IncompleteToolCall` is listed as a distinct failure-matrix entry with a mandatory symbol-presence assertion, but this symbol does not appear anywhere in the rebar codebase (`src/`, `tests/`), in the pydantic-ai import surface used by rebar (`pydantic_ai.exceptions`, `pydantic_ai.usage`, `pydantic_ai.models.function`), or in any documented pydantic-ai 1.107.0 public API. No import of this name exists anywhere in the project.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact pydantic-ai v1.x class(es) to import/raise for the 'structured provider content-filter refusal' and 'incomplete-tool-call error' matrix entries (or explicitly state these are injected as canned `FunctionModel` responses via `finish_reason`/response shape, not by raising a named exception class), and verify those symbols exist in `pydantic-ai-slim>=1,<2` as pinned in pyproject.toml.
findings.finding: The plan names 'a structured provider content-filter refusal' and 'an incomplete-tool-call error' as failure-matrix entries injected by 'raising the matching pydantic-ai exception types' imported from `pydantic_ai.exceptions`, but neither the concrete class names nor their existence in pydantic-ai v1.x's public API are verified anywhere in the codebase. The only confirmed pydantic-ai exception imports in this repo are `UsageLimitExceeded` (runner.py:178) and `ModelHTTPError` (PoC). If the implementer must raise a named class for these two cases and guesses a non-existent name (e.g. `ContentFilterError`, `IncompleteToolCall`), the test file fails at collection time and AC#1 ('make test runs it green on TODAY's code') cannot be met.
findings.location: Scope section â€” 'a structured provider content-filter refusal, an incomplete-tool-call error' bullet; AC#1
findings.suggested_fix: For the content-filter case, the injection is already handled via canned `finish_reason='content_filter'` on the FunctionModel response (confirmed at runner.py:483 and structured.py:219) â€” no import needed. For incomplete-tool-call, pydantic-ai raises `UnexpectedModelBehavior` (referenced in docs/adr/0006-llm-stage-seam-contracts.md and docs/experiments/structured-output-research.md); verify by running `python -c "from pydantic_ai.exceptions import UnexpectedModelBehavior"` in the dev venv and update the plan to name these symbols explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the hung-model/hung-tool guard mechanism (e.g., `@pytest.mark.timeout(N)` with `pytest-timeout`, or a `threading.Timer`-based approach with a concrete timeout value in seconds) and add an AC or inline note clarifying what the test asserts as evidence of unbounded behavior (e.g., the guard fires, not the runner's own timeout).
findings.finding: The 'hung model' and 'hung tool' liveness scenarios assert current unbounded behavior 'via a short outer test-level guard' but the mechanism for this guard is not described â€” no pytest plugin (e.g., pytest-timeout), threading approach, or signal-based timeout is specified. Asserting that something hangs (rather than times out on the happy path) is non-trivial and the implementation path is left to the executor.
findings.location: Scope section: 'a hung model request and a hung tool document that TODAY there is no per-request/per-tool timeout (the RED test asserts the current unbounded/hanging behavior via a short outer test-level guard)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet: 'The hung-model fixture uses a `FunctionModel` that blocks indefinitely (e.g., `time.sleep(9999)`); the test is decorated `@pytest.mark.timeout(2)` (pytest-timeout) and asserts that the test raises `pytest.raises(TimeoutError)` or exits via the mark â€” confirming no runner-internal timeout fires first. The guard timeout value is 2 s.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete outer timeout mechanism for liveness/hung-model test cases (e.g., `@pytest.mark.timeout(N)` via pytest-timeout, or a `threading.Timer`-based cancellation) and add it to the AC so the implementation is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan's liveness scenarios assert 'current unbounded/hanging behavior via a short outer test-level guard' for hung model/tool requests, but does not specify what mechanism provides this outer guard â€” leaving it ambiguous whether the test would actually hang CI if the guard is implemented incorrectly.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Named liveness scenarios pin CURRENT (pre-change) behavior only: a hung model request and a hung tool document that TODAY there is no per-request/per-tool timeout (the RED test asserts the current unbounded/hanging behavior via a short outer test-level guard)'

Alternatively, cite the pydantic-ai 1.107.0 exception hierarchy and map each case to a specific class.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description of the meta-test (its location, mechanism for enumerating parametrized IDs, and how it checks for `# CURRENT(` markers) to the What or Scope section so AC2's second clause has a covered deliverable in the plan body.
findings.finding: AC2 contains a second deliverable â€” a meta-test that asserts a `# CURRENT(` marker exists for every parametrized case â€” that is not described anywhere in the plan body (What or Scope), making it an orphan criterion with no implementation guidance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2; 'What' section; 'Scope' section
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add a bullet such as: 'A meta-test (e.g., a `test_current_markers` function in the same file or a conftest helper) reads the source of `test_llm_failure_matrix.py` and asserts that every `@pytest.mark.parametrize` ID has a corresponding `# CURRENT(` inline comment in the test body; the check uses `ast` or simple regex over the file text.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the `# CURRENT(file:line)` convention (its purpose, how later stories update it, and that line numbers may drift) in CLAUDE.md or a testing guide, and consider whether the meta-test should also grep the cited file:line to emit a warning when the referenced line no longer contains the expected token.
findings.finding: The `# CURRENT(file:line)` inline citation convention is a novel testing contract that later stories depend on to identify which assertions to flip, but it is undocumented outside this plan. The meta-test only checks that a `# CURRENT(` marker exists per parametrized id â€” it does NOT validate that the cited file:line is still accurate as the codebase evolves. Line numbers will silently drift when new lines are inserted above the cited locations, causing the 'regression net' guidance to point at wrong code without any automated signal.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria â€” second bullet; Scope â€” 'Each parametrized case carries an inline # CURRENT(file:line) comment'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to CLAUDE.md explaining the CURRENT-marker contract: what it means, that line numbers are informational (not enforced), and that story authors should re-verify the cited location before flipping an assertion. Optionally, enhance the meta-test to open the cited file, read the cited line, and assert it contains a recognizable token (e.g., 'LLMUnavailableError' or 'except Exception') so drift is caught automatically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconsider the meta-test that inspects source for `# CURRENT(` markers per parametrize id â€” it has no Rule-of-Three justification and requires bespoke source-scanning logic absent from the codebase; consider whether the inline comment convention alone (without a meta-test enforcement mechanism) is sufficient, or promote the marker-checking logic to a reusable fixture if it will serve â‰Ą3 future test files.
findings.finding: AC#2 mandates 'a meta-test asserts a `# CURRENT(` marker exists for each parametrized id' â€” a bespoke source-file inspection mechanism (reading the test file's own source text and scanning for comment strings keyed to parametrize ids) that has zero existing precedent in the entire test suite. This is a premature single-use abstraction: it has no Rule-of-Three support and rebuilds ad-hoc source-introspection logic the codebase does not otherwise contain.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #2: 'a meta-test asserts a `# CURRENT(` marker exists for each parametrized id'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fixture (e.g., `autouse` session- or module-scoped pytest fixture with `yield` + reset) that sets `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False` before each relevant test and restores it to its prior value in teardown, and name this fixture in the AC.
findings.finding: The `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False` global flag is set to guard tests, but the plan does not specify who sets it, when it is reset/restored, or how it is scoped to avoid leaking into other test modules that run in the same process.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`' / AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a pytest fixture in the test file (or conftest.py):
```python
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def block_model_requests():
    import pydantic_ai.models as m
    prev = m.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS
    m.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False
    yield
    m.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = prev
```
Reference this fixture in the plan and AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extract `_function_model` / `_sequence_model` (and the new raising-model variant) into a shared `tests/unit/conftest.py` or a `tests/unit/fixtures/llm_models.py` helper rather than adding a third private copy in the new file (Rule-of-Three threshold is now met: `test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:26`, `test_runner_hardening.py:23`, and the new file).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a third independent copy of the `_function_model` / `_sequence_model` FunctionModel factory helpers, which already exist identically in `tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:26-52` and `tests/unit/test_runner_hardening.py:23-36`. With three call-sites this now clears the Rule-of-Three threshold for promotion to a shared conftest fixture, yet the plan proposes another private copy instead of deduplicating.
findings.location: Scope section (new file `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py`) / What section (FunctionModel injection)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `_raising_function_model(exc)` factory and promote all three variants to `tests/unit/conftest.py` (or a `tests/unit/fixtures/pai_helpers.py` imported by all three files), so each test file imports from the shared module rather than redefining inline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the in-file `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False` guard from the plan's spec for the new file â€” `tests/conftest.py:260` already applies it session-wide as an autouse fixture; note in the file's docstring that it relies on the suite-wide conftest guard rather than re-establishing it.
findings.finding: The plan states 'Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`' as if the new test file must establish this guard, but `tests/conftest.py:247-260` already sets it as a session-scoped `autouse=True` fixture covering the entire test suite. Redeclaring it in the new file would be NIH reinvention of an already-present global safeguard.
findings.location: What section: 'Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`' / AC#1
findings.suggested_fix: In the new file's module docstring, add: `# Live-model guard: ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS=False is set suite-wide by tests/conftest.py:_no_live_model_requests` and remove any in-file assignment.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
